title: Canada, China Slash EV, Canola Tariffs in Reset of Ties
content: Canada and China struck an initial trade deal that will slash tariffs on electric vehicles and canola, as both nations promised to tear down trade barriers while forging new strategic ties during Prime Minister Mark Carney's visit. Canada is to allow Chinese EV imports at a 6.1% tariff, and expects China to lower the canola tariff. Canada welcomes further Chinese investment, and Trump expresses support for the deal. The first Canadian prime minister to visit China since 2017, Carney is seeking to rebuild ties with his country's second-largest trading partner following months of diplomatic efforts.
author: Analysts Account
